observations.recommendation: We recommend that strict compliance with the provisions of the RIRR of RA 9184 be observed by the BAC and management to prevent the possible invalidity of the resulting contract.
observations.managementAction: The agency signified strict compliance with the provisions of RA 9184.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the BAC, with the assistance of the BAC Secretariat, prepares the Annual Procurement Plan, duly supported by the Project Procurement Management Plan (PPMP) of each department/office and the Procurement Monitoring Report (PMR) as required in the Revised Implementing Rules and Regulations of RA 9184
observations.managementAction: Management will comply with the audit recommendations.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the conduct of educational tours/lakbayaral be made strictly in accordance with the pertinent memorandum circulars issued by the DILG.  Prudence should be exercise at all times in the use of LGU funds.  We also recommend for the refund of the excessive travelling expenses of P167,328.00 by the participants of the educational tour to Batangas and Oriental Mindoro.  Finally, we recommend for the immediate submission of each barangay’s Annual Investment Plan and/or capacity development plan for CYs 2013 and 2014.
observations.managementAction: Management will comply with the audit recommendations.
observations.reason: Disbursements for Lakbay Aral were no longer incurred by the LGU.  However, only minimal refunds were made by the concerned officials in CY 2016.

observations.recommendation: We recommend for the immediate submission of the lacking disbursement vouchers with its supporting documents by the Municipal Accountant.  We also recommend for the complete submission of the monthly accounts to avoid the suspension of the transactions in audit and the possible filing of a criminal/administrative case for Malversation of Public Funds and Property and Infidelity in the custody of documents against the concerned officials
observations.reason: The motorcycles remain unrecorded as of December 31, 2015.  No vouchers were submitted to the team.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that management strictly adhere to the pertinent provisions of COA rules in the recording of purchased supplies and materials to have a fairly presented inventory accounts in the financial statements at a given period.
observations.managementAction: Management informed about conduct of an inventory on agency assets thru the GSO. The results were already submitted to the audit team, however such are yet to be reconciled with the accounting records for these to be updated as certain items needs removal from the inventory due to obsolescence. The agency commits to rectify and adhere to the recommendations.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that regardless of urgency of need, management shall strictly direct adherence with the required payment of checks directly to payees except for small value items that could be paid out of petty cash fund. On the other hand, the practice of reimbursements shall be discourage, in effect promote the policy on payment on the basis of adequate documentation. This course of action has to be carried out as payments for reimbursements are without legal basis thus could be out rightly suspended or disallowed in audit depending on the presence of other infirmities per each transaction.
observations.managementAction: Management commented that, as discussed the audit recommendations will be implemented immediately.
observations.reason: Procurements through reimbursement were still noted for small value items.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that management strictly adhere to the mandates of the procurement law and its implementing rules regardless of the items or services sought to be acquired. In all instances, avoid splitting of purchase orders since wholly invalid.
observations.managementAction: Management will comply with the audit recommendations.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that management strictly direct conformity with the processes involved in the adoption of any of the alternative mode of procurement to ensure that utilization of funds are at the most economical manner, said undertaking to be duly proven thru complete documentations. in the case of procurement through shopping the attachment f duly filled-up and signed documents such as BAC Resolutions , Stock Availability Inquiry and Certification on occurrence of unforeseen contingency, among others.
observations.managementAction: Management commented that they have partially complied by attaching BAC Resolution along with the submitted disbursement vouchers to COA. The agency duly signified adherence to the rules and regulations on procurement.

observations.recommendation:
We recommend that management:

Strictly observe and adhere to the provisions of Section 10 of the NGAS Manual, Volume II and other related rules and regulations related to the maintenance of the Accounts Payable account for easy confirmation as to their validity and existence.
Instruct the Municipal Accountant to maintain individual subsidiary ledgers for each creditor with totals reconciled with the controlling account in the general ledger likewise submit all supporting DVs proving the existence f valid creditors concerning certified obligations for CY 2012, otherwise said payables should be reverted back to the appropriate equity account.
observations.managementAction: Agency management adheres to the audit recommendations. Currently account from prior years up to 2009 was already reverted to the equity accounts. The accounting department is collating the supporting documents of the current year’s accounts payable for the preparation of individual ledger cards.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that management strictly direct the adherence to the policy on no payment if without the required documents specified in COA Circular 2012-001. Specifically, checking on complete documentations by the Accountant of transactions referred tohis office shall according to actually existing supporting papers attached to DVs, box b of which will only be signed if all documentary proofs are present. In turn, release of funds by the treasurer shall solely be to DVs bearing the signature of Accountant under box
observations.managementAction:
Management has implemented the audit recommendation with regards to the completeness of supporting documents to prove the validity of the transactions.

The attention of the GSO and other departments concerned have been called regarding the compliance of the recommendation.
observations.reason: There are still disbursement transactions in CY 2016 with incomplete documentation as contained in Part II of this report.

observations.recommendation:
We recommend that management strictly adhere to the provisions of COA Circular No. 2012-002 pertaining to the accounting and reporting guidelines for the LDRRMF of LGUs and shall specifically undertake the ff:

Consistently provide for an allocation for 30% Quick Response Fund as stand-by fund for relief and recovery projects and activities.
Instruct the municipal accountant to cause the transfer of the CY 2012 unexpended balance of DRRMF – MOOE totalling P4,621,229.35 t the special trust fund under the account “trust liability DRRM (Code 438) in the trust fund books. This is to assure readily available funds in case of occurrence of disaster. Subsidiary ledgers for transfer shall be duly provided.
observations.managementAction: Concerned departments will conduct compliance discussions regarding this.
observations.reason: A similar observation was noted in CY 2016 as contained in Part II of this report.

observations.recommendation:
We recommend that the LCE :

Instruct to submit the aforementioned DVs;
Instruct the municipal accountant to record the remaining debit memos in the books with explanation as to particulars and other items that need adjustments, such as credit memos, deposits and interest income as presented in the bank reconciliation statements; and
Initiate administrative action against municipal treasurer if he still fails to submit the missing DVs.
observations.managementAction: The accounting office already records debit memos in the books and effected adjustments in the books on reconciling items appearing in the bank statements.
observations.reason: A similar observation was noted in CY 2016 as contained in Part II of this report.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the LCE should instruct officials concerned to strictly observe the guidelines in the utilization of the 20% development fund pursuant to DILG/DBM Joint Memorandum Circular No. 1 s.2005 and DILG Memorandum Circular No 2010-138 dated December 2, 2010. Henceforth for CY 2012transactions comply with DILG DBM Joint Circular No 2011-1 dated April 13, 2011.
observations.managementAction: The agency duly signified strict adherence to the guidelines in the utilization of the 20% development fund pursuant to DILG/DBM Joint Memorandum Circular No. 2010-138
observations.reason:
Some projects were unimplemented due to financial constraints.

A similar observation was noted in CY 2016 as contained in Part II of this Report.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the municipal accountant should draw a journal entry voucher to take up the cost of livestock under the livestock and crops inventory account pursuant to section 50, NGAS Manual for LGUs, volume III. Likewise, strictly comply Article 450 of the IRR of RA 7160 on the remittance of collections from sale of livestock and in no case shall such collections be used to purchase piglets for dispersal.
observations.managementAction: Still has to validate the whereabouts of the livestock with the office of the Municipal Agriculture.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the management should demand from the stallholders with delinquent payments on stall rentals t pay their accounts immediately and strictly impose the provisions of the Municipal Omnibus Tax Ordinance of 2006.
observations.managementAction: Management already demanded and collected payments from some of the stallholders with delinquent payment on stall rentals.

observations.recommendation: The LCE should instruct the municipal assessor to coordinate with and to request from the municipal accountant the list of all land, buildings and land improvements constructed/acquired by the municipality.  Conduct ocular inspection of these properties to validate existence, utilization and condition for issuance of DRPs for correction of information thereat.  Preparation and issuance of DRPs should be prioritized.
observations.managementAction: Assessor and accounting office started the verification of the required documents for the titling of the properties but were unable to pursue it due to the long tedious process.
observations.reason: No. DRPs were presented to the audit team

observations.recommendation: The LCE should instruct the municipal planning and development officer, municipal engineer, municipal accountant and the ABC President who will serve as representative of the barangay to coordinate in formulating written policies and guidelines relative to the granting of financial assistance to barangays.
observations.managementAction: The engineering office conducted monitoring on project funds out of financial assistance granted to barangays.

observations.recommendation: The municipal accountant should prepare adjusting entry to adjust the fixed assets and government equity account and for proper classification of the newly acquired property and equipment.
observations.managementAction: Has started verifying the affected asset accounts and the related supporting documents after which an adjusting entry shall be drawn.
